title: micrococca capensis
intro: Micrococca capensis, also known as the 'Bushman's poison' or 'Cape yellow-wood', is a small, deciduous shrub native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its roots and bark containing compounds that exhibit medicinal properties, particularly anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Micrococca capensis include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as supporting the immune system. Micrococca capensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional African medicine.
